findings.summary: useSettingsModal and useMediaGalleryHandlers use untyped CustomEvents on window ('openSettings', 'selectShotForAddition') to communicate across modules. These events bypass React's data flow, have no type-checked contracts, and create invisible coupling between distant components.
evidence: useSettingsModal.ts:27 — window.addEventListener('openSettings', handler as EventListener) with untyped CustomEvent detail
useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:107 — window.addEventListener('selectShotForAddition', handleSelectShotForAddition as EventListener) with manual cast
findings.suggestion: Replace window CustomEvents with a typed event emitter or React context callback. At minimum, define shared event-detail types so producers and consumers have a compile-time contract.

findings.summary: In useDeviceInfo, isTouchDevice is computed via useMemo depending only on [isMobile], but computeIsTouchDevice() reads window APIs directly and never uses isMobile. The value won't update when pointer capability changes unless isMobile also happens to change. The standalone useIsTouchDevice hook correctly uses resize listener but useDeviceInfo doesn't use it.
evidence: useMobile.tsx:168-171 — useMemo(() => computeIsTouchDevice(), [isMobile]) where computeIsTouchDevice (line 40-43) reads hasCoarsePointer/isTabletUA/isIpadOsLike without using isMobile
useMobile.tsx:116-130 — standalone useIsTouchDevice properly listens to resize events
findings.suggestion: Use the standalone useIsTouchDevice() hook inside useDeviceInfo instead of the useMemo, or add a resize/pointer-change listener to keep isTouchDevice reactive.

findings.summary: ShotActions accepts 22 individual props including multiple raw state setters and fine-grained boolean flags. This is a prop-drilling surface that makes the component hard to reason about and fragile to change.
evidence: ShotActions.tsx:14-47 — ShotActionsProps interface has 22 props spanning image data, mobile flags, selector state, loading IDs, boolean status flags, quick-create state object, and 6 callbacks
findings.suggestion: Group related props into objects (e.g., a shotActionState bag and a callbacks bag), or move some of this logic into a dedicated hook/context that ShotActions consumes directly.

findings.summary: The 'retro' and 'retro-secondary' button variants use extensive hardcoded hex colors (#e8e4db, #3a4a4a, #6a8a8a, etc.) instead of theme tokens, violating the project convention of never hardcoding colors.
evidence: button.tsx:21 — retro variant uses bg-[#e8e4db], dark:bg-[#3a4a4a], border-[#6a8a8a], text-[#5a7a7a], etc.
button.tsx:22 — retro-secondary variant uses similar hardcoded hex values
findings.suggestion: Extract these colors into CSS custom properties (e.g., --retro-bg, --retro-border) defined in the theme, then reference them as bg-retro, border-retro, etc.

findings.summary: ShotActions uses hardcoded color classes (bg-green-500, bg-gray-500/60) for state indication instead of theme tokens.
evidence: ShotActions.tsx:154 — 'bg-green-500 hover:bg-green-600 !text-white'
ShotActions.tsx:156 — 'bg-gray-500/60 hover:bg-gray-600/70 !text-white'
ShotActions.tsx:197 — 'bg-gray-500/80 hover:bg-gray-600/90 text-white'
findings.suggestion: Use semantic theme tokens (e.g., bg-success, bg-muted) or define custom properties for these states.

findings.summary: useMediaGalleryHandlers uses a setTimeout(100ms) to sequence setting state and closing the lightbox, which is a timing-dependent hack that can race with React batching.
evidence: useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:90-93 — setTimeout(() => { setShowTaskDetailsModal(true); setActiveLightboxMedia(null); }, 100)
findings.suggestion: Use a state machine or a single combined state update to avoid timing-dependent sequencing. If the delay is to allow an animation to complete, use an onAnimationEnd callback instead.

findings.summary: InstallInstructionsModal's browser mockup components use ~91 instances of hardcoded gray/color classes (bg-gray-100, bg-gray-800, text-gray-400, bg-red-400, bg-yellow-400, bg-green-400, bg-blue-500, etc.) rather than theme tokens.
evidence: InstallInstructionsModal.tsx — Pervasive hardcoded grays throughout all mockup components (TrafficLights, IPhoneNotch, all browser chrome components)
findings.suggestion: For the browser chrome mockups these may be intentionally non-themed (they represent real browser UI, not app UI), so this is a lower-confidence finding. If they should match the app theme, extract to theme tokens. If intentional, add a comment explaining why.

findings.summary: VisibilityManager is a fully implemented singleton (268 lines) that is never imported by any production code. Only referenced in browser-extensions.d.ts and its own test file. This is dead code that adds maintenance burden.
evidence: Grep for 'import.*VisibilityManager' across src/ returns only type declarations and test files
No production module imports or uses the VisibilityManager singleton
findings.suggestion: Delete VisibilityManager.ts and its test, or integrate it into the codebase (e.g., replace the manual visibilitychange listener in JoinClipsPage's useRefreshOnVisibility).

findings.summary: generationTaskBridge.ts defines GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces and the getTaskIdsForGenerations batch function that are only used internally within the file or in tests. enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData is only consumed by GenerationTaskContext (itself a wrapper). The batch function and types add abstraction without external consumers.
evidence: GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces are defined but never imported/used outside the file
getTaskIdsForGenerations is only called within preloadGenerationTaskMappings in the same file
enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData is only used by GenerationTaskContext.tsx
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused interface types. Consider whether the batch preload and enhance functions still serve a purpose or can be simplified.

findings.summary: LoRA validation logic (path required, strength 0-2) is duplicated verbatim between validateImageGenerationParams and validateBatchImageGenerationParams in imageGeneration.ts.
evidence: Lines 160-169 and lines 195-204 contain identical lora validation loops
Both throw the same TaskValidationError with the same message format
findings.suggestion: Extract a validateLoras(loras) helper function and call it from both validators.

findings.summary: useInlineEditState at 447 lines orchestrates 10+ hooks and manually threads individual properties between them via large builder functions (buildImageEditValueParams has 60+ lines of prop mapping, buildInlineEditStateResult has 60+ more). The resulting return type is a flat object with 50+ fields, making it fragile.
evidence: buildImageEditValueParams (lines 228-303) manually maps ~68 properties from 5 different hook results
buildInlineEditStateResult (lines 319-394) returns another ~60 properties
Total file is 447 lines of mostly wiring code
findings.suggestion: Consider grouping the return value into sub-objects by concern (e.g., inpainting, reposition, img2img, env) to reduce the flat surface area and make it easier to pass subsets to child components.

findings.summary: useProcessingTimestamp.ts and useUpdatingTimestamp.ts both implement relative time formatting with similar patterns (minutes/hours/days with singular/plural) but use completely separate formatting logic. useProcessingTimestamp hand-rolls duration math while useUpdatingTimestamp wraps date-fns formatDistanceToNow with string replacements.
evidence: formatProcessingDuration (lines 27-52) and formatCompletedTime (lines 57-94) in useProcessingTimestamp hand-roll minute/hour/day math
defaultAbbreviate (lines 20-52) in useUpdatingTimestamp uses string replacement on date-fns output
Both produce similar 'X mins ago' style strings with different approaches
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared formatRelativeDuration(diffMs, prefix) utility that both hooks can use, eliminating the parallel formatting implementations.

findings.summary: useInlineEditState accepts an _onClose parameter (prefixed with underscore indicating it's unused) but never uses it. This is a dead parameter in the public API.
evidence: Line 398: '_onClose: () => void' - underscore prefix convention indicates unused
No reference to _onClose or onClose anywhere in the function body
findings.suggestion: Remove the _onClose parameter from the function signature and update call sites.

findings.summary: usePaginatedTasks in useTasks.ts falls back to reading project ID from window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__, a global set by ProjectContext. This creates an implicit coupling outside the React tree and could cause stale reads.
evidence: Line 324: 'window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__?.selectedProjectId' used as fallback when projectId is not passed
ProjectContext.tsx line 73 sets this global in a useEffect
findings.suggestion: Require projectId to be explicitly passed or use the ProjectContext hook directly. The window global creates a hidden dependency that's hard to trace.

findings.summary: MediaGallery passes an extremely large number of props to sub-components (MediaGalleryGrid receives ~50+ props). The component at 635 lines is essentially a prop-routing orchestrator, making it difficult to understand data flow.
evidence: MediaGalleryGrid receives ~50 individual props (lines 472-565)
MediaGalleryLightbox receives ~40 individual props (lines 569-611)
useMediaGalleryActions receives ~20 individual props (lines 239-268)
findings.suggestion: Pass hook return objects directly to child components rather than destructuring and re-threading individual properties. For example, pass stateHook, filtersHook, paginationHook as grouped objects.

findings.summary: createImageGenerationTask uses IIFEs (immediately-invoked function expressions) inside object spread syntax for conditional property inclusion. This creates hard-to-read nested closures, especially the LoRA merging at lines 326-342 and reference settings at lines 344-362.
evidence: Lines 326-342: IIFE inside spread to conditionally build additional_loras map
Lines 344-362: IIFE with nested ternary for style reference settings
Lines 374-386: Another IIFE to merge per-phase LoRA overrides
findings.suggestion: Build the params object in stages with named intermediate variables (e.g., buildLoraMap(), buildReferenceParams()) instead of inline IIFEs. This improves readability and debuggability.

findings.summary: VideoLightbox uses a flat props interface (~40 individual props) while ImageLightbox groups them into sub-interfaces (navigation, shotWorkflow, features, actions). This inconsistency between the two sibling components makes the API harder to learn and maintain.
evidence: ImageLightboxProps uses grouped sub-interfaces: navigation?: LightboxNavigationProps, shotWorkflow?: LightboxShotWorkflowProps, features?: LightboxFeatureFlags, actions?: LightboxActionHandlers
VideoLightboxProps has all ~40 props at the top level: onNext, onPrevious, showNavigation, hasNext, hasPrevious, allShots, selectedShotId, etc.
findings.suggestion: Align VideoLightboxProps to use the same grouped pattern as ImageLightboxProps, potentially sharing the same sub-interfaces.

findings.summary: useTasks.ts uses both 'import React from react' and destructured hooks pattern inconsistently. React.useRef and React.useEffect are used on lines 352-358 instead of destructured imports, while the file also uses named imports from react-query.
evidence: Line 1: 'import React from "react"' but no destructured hook imports
Lines 352-358: 'React.useRef', 'React.useEffect' instead of importing useRef/useEffect directly
Other files in the codebase consistently use destructured imports
findings.suggestion: Switch to destructured imports: import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react' for consistency with the rest of the codebase.

findings.summary: useToggleGenerationStar uses window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('generation-star-updated')) to notify the Timeline of star updates. This is a side-channel that bypasses React's data flow, making it invisible to type checking and hard to trace.
evidence: Lines 281-283: CustomEvent dispatch on onSuccess callback
No TypeScript enforcement that listeners exist or handle the correct payload shape
findings.suggestion: Consider using React Query's onMutate/onSettled with targeted invalidation instead of a custom DOM event, or at least type the event payload and document the consumer.

findings.summary: GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces are defined but never used outside the file, and getTaskIdForGeneration/getTaskIdsForGenerations are private yet the comment block lists them as centralized consolidation targets.
evidence: Lines 25-35: interfaces GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping are declared but no code references them (grep confirms zero external usage)
getTaskIdsForGenerations is only called internally by preloadGenerationTaskMappings; getTaskIdForGeneration is only used by useGetTaskIdForGeneration
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused interfaces. They add noise and false promise of a richer API surface.

findings.summary: PendingClipAction.type ('fill' | 'append') is set in consumePendingJoinClips but never read by applyPendingClipActions, which always tries empty-slot-first regardless of the type field.
evidence: Line 165: type is assigned based on filledCount
Lines 190-213: applyPendingClipActions iterates actions but never reads action.type - it unconditionally searches for the first empty index
findings.suggestion: Remove the type field from PendingClipAction since the merge logic is position-based, not type-based. This eliminates a misleading contract.

findings.summary: extractTaskId in generationTransformers declares it accepts `string[] | string | null` but silently drops a plain string value, returning null instead of the string itself.
evidence: Line 241: parameter type is `tasks: string[] | string | null | undefined`
Lines 242-246: only `Array.isArray` path extracts a value; a plain string falls through to `return null`
findings.suggestion: Add `if (typeof tasks === 'string') return tasks;` before the array check, or narrow the type to exclude the impossible case.

findings.summary: The pattern of reading Supabase auth tokens from localStorage by parsing the project ref from SUPABASE_URL is duplicated in at least three files (taskCreation.ts, toolSettingsService.ts, invokeWithTimeout.ts) with slightly different shapes.
evidence: taskCreation.ts lines 17-28: readAccessTokenFromStorage extracts access_token
toolSettingsService.ts lines 60-71: readUserIdFromStorage extracts user.id
Both parse `sb-${projectRef}-auth-token` from localStorage with identical URL parsing
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `readSessionFromStorage()` utility that returns the full parsed session object, then each caller picks the field it needs.

findings.summary: generateVideoService.test.ts contains a single trivial assertion (exports expected members) with `expect(true).not.toBe(false)` padding. The 650-line service with complex branching (timeline vs batch, phase config validation, structure guidance, pair overrides) has no meaningful test coverage.
evidence: Test file lines 6-12: single test that only checks buildBasicModePhaseConfig is defined, plus two `expect(true).not.toBe(false)` no-ops
Source file contains pure functions like buildImagePayload, buildTimelinePairConfig, buildBatchPairConfig, resolveModelPhaseSelection, validatePhaseConfigConsistency - all testable without mocks
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests for the pure helper functions (buildImagePayload, buildTimelinePairConfig, buildBatchPairConfig, resolveModelPhaseSelection, validatePhaseConfigConsistency). These are extract-and-test friendly.

findings.summary: supabaseTypeHelpers.ts has no test file. While the function is trivial (a type cast), it represents an explicit boundary cast that the module comment says should be trackable.
evidence: Glob for supabaseTypeHelpers test returned no results
findings.suggestion: Low priority given the function is a single-line cast, but a smoke test documenting the contract would be consistent with the stated intent.

findings.summary: fetchLineageChain makes one Supabase query per ancestor in a while loop (N+1 pattern). For deep lineage chains this could produce many sequential round-trips.
evidence: Lines 42-71: while loop issues `supabase.from('generation_variants').select(...).eq('id', currentId).single()` per iteration
Each iteration is a sequential network round-trip; a chain of depth 5 means 5 queries
findings.suggestion: Consider an RPC or recursive CTE that fetches the full chain in one query. The 5-minute staleTime mitigates impact but doesn't eliminate it for first loads.

findings.summary: generateClientThumbnail wraps callback-based Image/FileReader loading in a manual Promise constructor. This is functional but the nested callbacks (reader.onload -> img.onload -> canvas.toBlob) create deep nesting that is harder to follow.
evidence: Lines 29-112: single Promise constructor with 3 levels of callback nesting (FileReader -> Image -> canvas.toBlob)
findings.suggestion: Break into smaller promise-returning helpers (e.g., readFileAsDataUrl, loadImage, canvasToBlob) and compose with async/await to flatten the nesting.

findings.summary: generateVideoService.ts is 654 lines with deeply nested config-building functions. While it is already well-decomposed into helper functions, buildTravelRequestBody alone takes 24 parameters via a params object, making it fragile to extend.
evidence: Lines 437-529: buildTravelRequestBody takes a params object with 24 fields
Lines 536-653: generateVideo orchestrator is 117 lines with multiple sequential try/catch blocks
findings.suggestion: Consider grouping related params into sub-objects (e.g., ImagePayload, PairConfig already exist - extend this pattern to model/phase/prompt groups) to reduce the parameter surface of buildTravelRequestBody.

findings.summary: timelineWriteQueue.test.ts is colocated with the source file instead of in a __tests__ directory, inconsistent with the project convention used by every other test file in the reviewed set.
evidence: File is at src/shared/lib/timelineWriteQueue.test.ts while all other tests are in __tests__ subdirectories (e.g., src/shared/lib/__tests__/generationTaskBridge.test.ts)
findings.suggestion: Move to src/shared/lib/__tests__/timelineWriteQueue.test.ts for consistency.

findings.summary: performanceUtils.ts is completely empty (only a doc comment, no exports). It has zero importers across the codebase and serves no purpose.
evidence: File body is a single doc comment with no code/exports
Grep for 'performanceUtils' across src returns zero import hits
findings.suggestion: Delete the file entirely.

findings.summary: useRenderLogger has an empty else-if branch at line 50-51. The periodic render-count log was removed but the conditional skeleton was left behind.
evidence: Line 50-51: `} else if (debugConfig.isEnabled('renderLogging') && (total <= 3 || total % 25 === 0)) {\n    }`
findings.suggestion: Remove the empty else-if block entirely; the loop-detect branch above is the only active path.

findings.summary: useChangedDepsLogger computes a `changed` object at line 78-82 but never uses it (no console.log or return). The logging statement was removed but the computation was left.
evidence: Lines 78-83: `changed` record is built with a for loop but never consumed
findings.suggestion: Either restore the log statement or remove the dead computation. The ref update at line 86 is still needed.

findings.summary: The deprecated shim at tools/travel-between-images/hooks/useSegmentOutputsForShot.ts still has 4 consumers importing through it instead of the canonical '@/shared/hooks/segments' path.
evidence: Shim file says '@deprecated Import from @/shared/hooks/segments instead'
FinalVideoSection.tsx:22: import from '../hooks/useSegmentOutputsForShot'
useShotImagesEditorModel.ts:3: import from '../../../hooks/useSegmentOutputsForShot'
Timeline.tsx:18: import type SegmentSlot from '../hooks/useSegmentOutputsForShot'
useShotEditorController.ts:51: import from '../../hooks/useSegmentOutputsForShot'
findings.suggestion: Update the 4 consumers to import from '@/shared/hooks/segments' then delete the shim.

findings.summary: The deprecated re-export shim at tools/edit-video/components/VideoPortionEditor.tsx still has one consumer (VideoReplaceMode.tsx) importing through it.
evidence: Shim says '@deprecated Import from @/shared/components/VideoPortionEditor instead'
VideoReplaceMode.tsx:13: import { VideoPortionEditor } from './VideoPortionEditor'
findings.suggestion: Update VideoReplaceMode.tsx to import from '@/shared/components/VideoPortionEditor' then delete the shim.

findings.summary: The deprecated re-export shim at shared/hooks/useSegmentSettings.ts still has one consumer (useSegmentSettingsForm.ts) importing from it via relative path instead of '@/shared/hooks/segments'.
evidence: Shim says '@deprecated Import from @/shared/hooks/segments instead'
useSegmentSettingsForm.ts:28: import { useSegmentSettings, UseSegmentSettingsOptions } from './useSegmentSettings'
findings.suggestion: Update useSegmentSettingsForm.ts to import from './segments' or '@/shared/hooks/segments', then delete the shim.

findings.summary: ProjectImageSettings in ImageGenerationForm/types.ts has 7 deprecated fields (selectedLorasByTextModel, selectedReferenceId, styleReferenceImage, styleReferenceImageOriginal, styleReferenceStrength, subjectStrength, subjectDescription, inThisScene). Some are still read by migration/legacy code paths, but several appear to only exist in the type definition.
evidence: types.ts lines 97-114: 7 fields marked @deprecated
selectedLorasByTextModel used only in useLoraCategoryMigration.ts (migration hook)
styleReferenceImage, styleReferenceStrength, etc. used in useHydratedReferences.ts and projectSettingsInheritance.ts for legacy data compat
findings.suggestion: Audit whether these migration code paths still encounter legacy data. If migration is complete, remove both the fields and the migration code.

findings.summary: useSegmentSettingsForm returns two deprecated fields (persistedEnhancePromptEnabled, saveEnhancePromptEnabled) alongside their replacements (effectiveEnhanceEnabled, handleEnhancePromptChange). The deprecated fields have zero external consumers.
evidence: Lines 146-155: @deprecated persistedEnhancePromptEnabled and saveEnhancePromptEnabled
Grep shows persistedEnhancePromptEnabled only referenced in useSegmentSettingsForm.ts itself (declaration + return)
findings.suggestion: Remove the two deprecated fields from the return type and return object since no external consumer uses them.

findings.summary: MediaGallery/types.ts defines MetadataLora locally with a comment 'Define types here to avoid circular imports' (line 11), but MetadataLora has zero external importers - it is only used internally within this file. The type and its comment appear to be scaffolding that was never wired up.
evidence: Line 11: '// Define types here to avoid circular imports'
MetadataLora is only referenced at line 12 (definition) and line 57 (usage within same file)
Grep for 'import.*MetadataLora' across src returns zero hits
findings.suggestion: Remove the apologetic comment. The type is fine where it is, but the 'to avoid circular imports' rationale is misleading since nothing imports it from outside.

findings.summary: RawGeneration has a 'DEPRECATED' shot_generations field alongside the new shot_data JSONB format. The old join path is still actively used by 5+ files across the codebase, so this is not just a type definition issue but accumulated migration debt that was started and not finished.
evidence: generationTransformers.ts line 157-161: 'DEPRECATED: Old join format' but lines 344-345 still fallback to it
5+ files across src still access .shot_generations property (useDerivedItems, useExternalGenerations, useSourceGeneration, MediaGalleryLightbox, useImageGeneration, useTasksLightbox)
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration to shot_data JSONB format in all query sites, then remove the shot_generations field and fallback code.

findings.summary: MediaGalleryProps.onPrefetchAdjacentPages is marked @deprecated (use generationFilters instead) but is still passed through in VideoTravelVideosGallery and MediaGallery's internal plumbing.
evidence: MediaGallery/types.ts line 244-245: '@deprecated Use generationFilters instead'
VideoTravelVideosGallery.tsx line 53-54: still defines onPrefetchAdjacentPages in PreloadingProps with @deprecated
MediaGallery/index.tsx line 89: destructures onPrefetchAdjacentPages with comment '// Deprecated'
findings.suggestion: Remove onPrefetchAdjacentPages from all prop types and plumbing since the replacement (generationFilters) is already in use.

findings.summary: Error handling logic is scattered across four separate files at the same level in shared/lib: errorHandler.ts (re-export barrel), errors.ts (error classes), errorUtils.ts (type guards), and the errorHandling/ directory (handleError.ts, errorNormalization.ts, errorPresentation.ts). The barrel file errorHandler.ts re-exports from both errors.ts and errorHandling/handleError.ts, creating a confusing import graph where consumers use three different import paths for closely related functionality.
evidence: errorHandler.ts is a 10-line barrel that re-exports from errorHandling/handleError.ts and errors.ts
errorUtils.ts has overlapping abort-error detection with errors.ts (NetworkError.fromError also checks AbortError)
217 files import from errorHandling/handleError.ts directly while 6 files import from errorHandler.ts barrel, showing inconsistent import conventions
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into the errorHandling/ directory. Move errors.ts and errorUtils.ts into errorHandling/, update the barrel to re-export everything, and standardize on a single import path.

findings.summary: queryKeys.ts barrel file coexists alongside the queryKeys/ directory at the same level in shared/lib. The barrel re-exports all 12 sub-module key objects into a single queryKeys namespace, but consumers inconsistently import from either the barrel (queryKeys.ts) or the individual sub-modules (queryKeys/api.ts, etc.).
evidence: queryKeys.ts imports from 12 sub-modules in queryKeys/ directory
useApiKeys.ts imports from queryKeys/api.ts directly, bypassing the barrel
useApiKeys.test.ts mocks '@/shared/lib/queryKeys' (the barrel), not '@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api' (the actual import), causing the mock to target the wrong module
findings.suggestion: Move the barrel into queryKeys/index.ts so the file and directory share a single import path, eliminating the ambiguity.

findings.summary: Test files mock modules at wrong import paths, meaning the mocks do not actually intercept the real imports. useApiKeys.test.ts mocks '@/shared/lib/queryKeys' but the production code imports from '@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api'. useAIInteractionService.test.ts mocks '@/shared/lib/errorHandler' but the production code imports from '@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError'.
evidence: useApiKeys.test.ts line 51-58: vi.mock('@/shared/lib/queryKeys', ...) but useApiKeys.ts line 6: import { apiQueryKeys } from '@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api'
useAIInteractionService.test.ts line 8: vi.mock('@/shared/lib/errorHandler', ...) but useAIInteractionService.ts line 3: import { handleError } from '@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError'
findings.suggestion: Update mock paths in tests to match the actual import paths used by the production modules. The tests pass currently only because the mocked functions are not asserted against in ways that would reveal the mismatched mocks.

findings.summary: Five test files in shared/lib sit alongside their source files rather than in the __tests__/ subdirectory that already exists in the same directory. This is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses __tests__/ directories.
evidence: shared/lib/__tests__/ directory exists with many tests, but 5 test files sit at the shared/lib/ root level
shared/hooks/ consistently uses __tests__/ subdirectory for all tests
findings.suggestion: Move these 5 test files into shared/lib/__tests__/ for consistency.

findings.summary: src/shared/utils/ directory still exists with 7 source files and a __tests__/ directory, despite the project description stating the straggler src/utils/ was deleted. This is a second utils directory that parallels shared/lib functionality.
evidence: shared/lib/ (74 files) is the canonical location for non-hook shared code
shared/utils/ (8 files) exists as a parallel location with no clear distinction in purpose
findings.suggestion: Migrate the remaining shared/utils/ files into shared/lib/ and remove the directory to eliminate the ambiguity of two utility directories.

findings.summary: The pane-positioning/ subdirectory under shared/hooks uses kebab-case naming while all other subdirectories (gallery/, invalidation/, segments/, shots/, timeline/, etc.) use camelCase or single-word names. This is inconsistent with the recent renaming effort that converted kebab-case hook files to camelCase.
evidence: All other shared/hooks subdirectories: gallery, invalidation, segments, shots, sourceImageChanges, timeline, videoFrameExtraction, videoHoverPreview use camelCase or single words
pane-positioning uses kebab-case, the only subdirectory to do so
findings.suggestion: Rename to panePositioning/ for consistency with the codebase naming convention.

findings.summary: A README_timestamp_updates.md documentation file exists inside src/shared/hooks/, which is a code directory. This is a catalog/design doc that will drift from the actual implementation and violates the CLAUDE.md rule against creating catalog docs.
evidence: 114-line markdown file explaining the timestamp system architecture, usage examples, and component listing
Contains component inventory (lines 87-91) that can drift from source
CLAUDE.md states: 'Don't create catalog docs (hook lists, component inventories, file trees)'
findings.suggestion: Delete the README. The typed source (useTimestampUpdater.ts, useUpdatingTimestamp.ts) is the source of truth.

findings.summary: ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx hardcodes Tailwind color values (bg-slate-50/50, dark:bg-slate-800/30) instead of using semantic design tokens (bg-background, bg-muted, etc.) as required by the project conventions.
evidence: Line 52: bg-slate-50/50 dark:bg-slate-800/30
Line 68: bg-slate-50/50 dark:bg-slate-800/30
CLAUDE.md: 'never hardcode colors (use bg-background, text-foreground, etc.)'
findings.suggestion: Replace bg-slate-50/50 dark:bg-slate-800/30 with semantic tokens like bg-muted or bg-card.

findings.summary: useAIInteractionService accepts an apiKey option that is immediately aliased to _apiKey (underscore-prefixed = unused). The comment says 'now strictly relies on this being passed' but the parameter is never used in any of the three edge function calls. This is a dead parameter that misleads callers.
evidence: Line 12: apiKey?: string; // API key for the AI service - now strictly relies on this being passed.
Line 17-18: apiKey: _apiKey - destructured but prefixed with underscore (unused)
All three edge function calls (lines 30, 56, 105) use invokeWithTimeout without passing any API key
findings.suggestion: Remove the apiKey option from UseAIInteractionServiceOptions and update callers.

findings.summary: The generateSummary callback in useAIInteractionService is a useCallback wrapper around generateSummaryForPromptInternal that adds no logic - it simply calls and returns the internal function. The comment 'Expose a version that uses the hook's API key' is stale since the API key is unused.
evidence: Lines 128-134: generateSummary wraps generateSummaryForPromptInternal with zero additional logic
Comment on line 127 references 'the hook's API key' but apiKey is unused (_apiKey)
findings.suggestion: Expose generateSummaryForPromptInternal directly as generateSummary, or rename it and remove the wrapper.

findings.summary: Multiple comments in useAIInteractionService.ts reference outdated implementation details ('new edge function', 'new implementation', 'the hook's API key') suggesting the code was migrated from a client-side AI API approach but the comments were not fully cleaned up.
evidence: Line 29: '// Invoke the new edge function'
Line 48: '// === New implementation: delegate prompt generation to Supabase Edge Function ==='
Line 54: '// Invoke the new edge function'
Line 104: '// Invoke the new edge function'
Line 127: '// Expose a version of generateSummaryForPromptInternal that uses the hook's API key.'
findings.suggestion: Remove 'new' from comments since the edge function approach is now the established pattern, and fix the stale API key reference.

findings.summary: useApiKeys.ts implements a manual check-then-insert-or-update pattern (lines 42-73) that could be replaced with a single Supabase upsert call, reducing code by roughly 15 lines and eliminating a race condition between the existence check and the insert/update.
evidence: Lines 42-47: SELECT to check if user exists
Lines 49-58: INSERT if not exists
Lines 62-70: UPDATE if exists
Supabase supports .upsert() which handles this atomically
findings.suggestion: Replace the check-then-insert/update with a single supabase.from('users').upsert({ id: user.id, api_keys: apiKeys }).select('api_keys').single() call.

findings.summary: Three debug-related files (debugConfig.ts, debugPolling.ts, debugRendering.ts) sit at the shared/lib/ root level rather than in a debug/ subdirectory, mirroring the error handling fragmentation pattern.
evidence: Three files with 'debug' prefix at the same level, each handling a distinct debug concern
Other cross-cutting concerns (errorHandling, queryKeys, preloading, tasks) have their own subdirectories
findings.suggestion: Group into a debug/ subdirectory for consistency with other multi-file concerns.

findings.summary: The 'retro' and 'retro-secondary' button variants in button.tsx hardcode hex color values (e.g., #e8e4db, #3a4a4a, #6a8a8a) instead of using semantic CSS variables/Tailwind tokens. CLAUDE.md explicitly states 'never hardcode colors'.
evidence: Line 21: retro variant uses bg-[#e8e4db], border-[#6a8a8a], text-[#5a7a7a], etc.
Line 22: retro-secondary variant uses border-[#6a8a8a]/35, text-[#5a7a7a], etc.
CLAUDE.md states: 'never hardcode colors (use bg-background, text-foreground, etc.)'
findings.suggestion: Define retro color palette as CSS custom properties in the theme and reference them via Tailwind utility classes (e.g., bg-retro, border-retro-accent).

findings.summary: Mixed usage of process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' and import.meta.env.DEV across shared code. In a Vite project, import.meta.env.DEV is the canonical check; process.env.NODE_ENV is not guaranteed to be available at runtime.
evidence: imageGeneration.ts lines 474, 485: uses process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'
DataFreshnessManager.ts line 357: uses import.meta.env.DEV
7 files in src/shared use process.env.NODE_ENV, 10+ use import.meta.env.DEV
findings.suggestion: Standardize on import.meta.env.DEV for all Vite-compiled frontend code. process.env.NODE_ENV should only appear in test files or Node-only code.

findings.summary: Edge functions use three different Deno std versions (0.168.0, 0.177.0, 0.224.0) and three different supabase-js import schemes (esm.sh@2.39.7, esm.sh@2, npm:@supabase/supabase-js@2). This creates risk of subtle runtime differences between functions.
evidence: complete_task uses deno.land/std@0.224.0 + npm:@supabase/supabase-js@2
create-task uses deno.land/std@0.224.0 + esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2.39.7
delete-project uses deno.land/std@0.168.0 + esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2
trigger-auto-topup uses deno.land/std@0.168.0 + esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2
findings.suggestion: Standardize on a single Deno std version and a single supabase-js import scheme (preferably npm: specifier with the latest version) across all edge functions.

findings.summary: All shared auth functions (authenticateRequest, verifyTaskOwnership, getTaskUserId, verifyShotOwnership) type the supabaseAdmin parameter as 'unknown', then call .from()/.select() on it without any type assertion or type guard. This erases all type checking on the Supabase client usage within auth.
evidence: Line 51: authenticateRequest(req: Request, supabaseAdmin: unknown, ...)
Line 117: supabaseAdmin.from('user_api_tokens').select('user_id')... called on 'unknown'
Line 152: verifyTaskOwnership(supabaseAdmin: unknown, ...)
Line 277: getTaskUserId(supabaseAdmin: unknown, ...)
findings.suggestion: Import the SupabaseClient type from @supabase/supabase-js and type the parameter as SupabaseClient or a minimal interface with the .from() method signature.

findings.summary: The useToggleGenerationStar optimistic update casts shots cache data to Array<Record<string, unknown>> and nested images to Array<Record<string, unknown>>, losing all type safety on the Shot and GenerationRow structures being manipulated.
evidence: Line 233: (data as Array<Record<string, unknown>>).map((shot) => {
Line 237: (shot.images as Array<Record<string, unknown>>).map((img) => ...)
findings.suggestion: Import the Shot type and use proper type assertions (data as Shot[]) so property access is type-checked.

findings.summary: The createGeneration helper function returns Promise<Record<string, unknown>> instead of the actual generation row type, discarding all type information about the returned database row.
evidence: Line 66: async function createGeneration(params: {...}): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
findings.suggestion: Return the proper GenerationRow type or the Supabase-inferred row type from the .select().single() call.

findings.summary: GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces are defined but never used as type annotations anywhere in the module or its consumers.
evidence: Line 25: interface GenerationTaskMapping { ... } - defined but never referenced
Line 31: interface TaskGenerationMapping { ... } - defined but never referenced
Grep for these names shows they only appear in the definition file and test files
findings.suggestion: Remove unused interfaces or apply them as return types on the mapping functions (e.g., getTaskIdForGeneration should return GenerationTaskMapping).

findings.summary: usePaginatedTasks falls back to window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__?.selectedProjectId when projectId is not provided. This couples a hook to a hidden global, making data flow unclear and hard to trace.
evidence: Line 323-324: const effectiveProjectId: string | null = projectId ?? (typeof window !== 'undefined' ? window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__?.selectedProjectId ?? null : null);
findings.suggestion: Make projectId a required parameter or use the ProjectContext React hook directly instead of reading from a window global.

findings.summary: In createGeneration, if the generation_variants insert fails, the error is logged but the function still returns the generation as if everything succeeded. The caller has no way to know the variant was not created.
evidence: Lines 112-114: if (variantError) { console.error('[useGenerationMutations] Failed to create variant:', variantError); } -- then falls through to return data
findings.suggestion: Either throw the error so the mutation's onError handler can handle it, or include a warning in the return value so callers can detect partial failure.

findings.summary: complete_task does not pass allowJwtUserAuth: true to authenticateRequest, while create-task and most other user-facing edge functions do. If a browser-based user sends a JWT to complete_task, it will fail authentication despite the user being valid.
evidence: complete_task line 161: authenticateFn(req, supabaseAdmin, '[COMPLETE-TASK]') -- no allowJwtUserAuth option
create-task line 78: authenticateRequest(req, supabaseAdmin, '[CREATE-TASK]', { allowJwtUserAuth: true })
findings.suggestion: If complete_task is only called by workers (service-role or PAT), this is intentional and should be documented. If browser users can call it, add { allowJwtUserAuth: true }.

findings.summary: complete_task does not handle CORS preflight (OPTIONS) requests or include CORS headers in responses, while create-task and delete-project do. If complete_task is ever called from a browser context, it will fail preflight.
evidence: complete_task has no Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers and no OPTIONS handler
create-task lines 10-18, 32-39: has corsHeaders and OPTIONS preflight handling
findings.suggestion: If complete_task is worker-only (server-to-server), this is intentional but should be documented. Otherwise, add CORS handling consistent with other edge functions.

findings.summary: fetchTaskContext in complete_task/index.ts types the supabase parameter as 'unknown' and then calls .from().select() on it, which bypasses all Supabase type checking at the edge function level.
evidence: Line 37-38: async function fetchTaskContext(supabase: unknown, taskId: string)
Line 40: const { data: task, error } = await supabase.from('tasks').select(...)
findings.suggestion: Use the proper Supabase client type, same issue as _shared/auth.ts. This is a pattern-level issue across all edge functions.

findings.summary: The placeholderData callback uses a non-standard function signature. React Query v5's placeholderData expects either a value or a function of (previousData, previousQuery), but the inline arrow uses only one parameter with an explicit type annotation that may conflict with the actual TanStack Query generics.
evidence: Line 344: placeholderData: (previousData: PaginatedTasksResponse | undefined) => previousData
findings.suggestion: Use the built-in keepPreviousData import or let TypeScript infer the parameter type: placeholderData: (prev) => prev.

findings.summary: The complete_task catch block returns internal error messages and partial stack traces to the caller, which can leak implementation details.
evidence: Line 354: return new Response(`Internal error: ${error?.message}`, { status: 500 });
Line 304: return new Response(`Database update failed: ${dbError.message}`, { status: 500 });
findings.suggestion: Return generic error messages to the caller and log the details server-side only. Use the logger for detailed error info.

findings.summary: DataFreshnessManager shows a toast.success('Connection restored') on circuit breaker recovery, which contradicts the CLAUDE.md convention of 'Toasts: errors only (no success toasts for task creation)'.
evidence: Line 233-236: toast.success('Connection restored', { duration: 3000, id: 'network-recovery-toast' });
CLAUDE.md: 'Toasts: errors only'
findings.suggestion: This may be intentionally exempt from the 'errors only' rule since it's a network recovery notification (not task creation), but it should be explicitly documented as an exception or removed.

findings.summary: useDeviceInfo computes isTouchDevice via useMemo with [isMobile] as the only dependency, but computeIsTouchDevice reads from navigator and window which can change independently of isMobile (e.g., connecting a mouse to a tablet). The value may be stale.
evidence: Lines 168-171: const isTouchDevice = React.useMemo(() => { ... return computeIsTouchDevice(); }, [isMobile]);
findings.suggestion: Use the existing useIsTouchDevice hook (which subscribes to resize events) instead of the useMemo shortcut, or add resize event listener like the standalone hook does.

findings.summary: Minor quality variations in naming quality
evidence: Score reflects minor issues identified during review
findings.suggestion: Continue monitoring naming quality quality across the codebase
